content:
We have two TODOs related to adding WARN_ONCE support in the Rust side of the kernel tree:

// rust/kernel/mm/virt.rs
// TODO: call WARN_ONCE once the Rust version is available

// rust/kernel/error.rs
// TODO: Use `WARN_ONCE` once it's implemented

I am thinking to implement this along with support for other log levels (e.g., info, notice, emerg, etc.).

Right now, I am considering two possible approaches:

  1. Adding *_once macros for each log level:
pr_info_once!("Some info, just once");
  1. Extending the existing pr_* macros to optionally support once logging:
pr_info!("Some info");              // Standard usage
pr_info!("Some info", once = true); // Log once

I would be happy to hear any opinions on this, whether the first or second approach is better or if there is a better alternative altogether.
There is a series on list already: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20241126-pr_once_macros-v4-0-410b8ca9643e@tuta.io
As far as I see this was the latest update on that serie: https://lore.kernel.org/all/OIpcOXB--F-9@tuta.io/
Right, I remember now. I did not do anything about it, so feel free to pick up that series and send a new version.

Sure, first I need to read the whole series when I get some free time. I need to understand why we are abstracting OnceLite when we could simply use a static Once or AtomicBool inside the pr_*_once macros.
There is a separate discussion on Zulip too: #Help > warn_once/do_once_lite_if , will check both the patch series and the Zulip topic in a few days.
We don't have Once in the kernel yet. We are in the process of getting atomics, and I wrote a SetOnce based on the atomic series (related but not the same).
